title: Warm Analog Bass with FLEX
goal: Create a rounded, mono-safe analog-style bass suitable for trap.

steps.action: Load a subtractive bass preset and keep it dry — no reverb or delay on the low end.
steps.tip: Lower a brightness macro for a darker tone; illustrative cutoff around 30-40%.

steps.action: Shorten the envelope Release (~5-10%) so notes stay tight and do not overlap.
steps.tip: Check the bass in mono; wide bass loses power on club systems.

steps.action: High-pass rumble below the fundamental (Band 1) and gently lift the body around 100-200Hz.

title: Vocal Clarity with Fruity Parametric EQ 2
goal: Clean up and open a lead vocal without making it harsh.

steps.action: High-pass with Band 1 around 80Hz to remove rumble and free up headroom.
steps.tip: Never boost Band 1 unless you are designing bass.

steps.action: Cut low-mid mud around 200-300Hz (Band 2/3) with a moderate bandwidth.
steps.tip: Bandwidth is inverted here: lower % = wider.

steps.action: Add a gentle presence lift near 2-3kHz (Band 5) for intelligibility.
steps.tip: Presence stacks across tracks — overuse causes harshness.

steps.action: Optionally add a high-shelf 'air' around 10kHz (Band 7) for modern gloss.

title: FM Sub Bass with Sytrus
goal: Design a tuned, harmonically rich sub bass using FM.

steps.action: Start from a simple algorithm with one operator modulating a near-sine carrier.
steps.tip: Keep operator frequency in Ratio mode so the pitch stays tuned.

steps.action: Raise modulation amount gradually to add harmonics; keep the envelope attack near-instant and release short.
steps.tip: Small modulation changes shift timbre a lot — move slowly.

steps.action: High-pass sub-rumble and tame any harsh upper harmonic with a narrow cut.

title: Clean Vocal Chain
goal: Tune, de-clutter and control a lead vocal so it sits forward in the mix.

steps.action: Correct obvious pitch drift by note, keeping the contour natural rather than fully snapping.
steps.tip: Edit the pitch line inside a note before moving the whole note.

steps.action: High-pass below ~90Hz, dip boxiness around 300-500Hz, and add a gentle presence lift near 4-6kHz.
steps.tip: Cut before you boost - clearing mud often removes the need for a big top-end lift.

steps.action: Use the compressor stage for gentle levelling, then a touch of limiting to hold peaks.
steps.tip: Aim for a few dB of gain reduction, not a squashed sound.

title: Trance Gate Lead
goal: Turn a sustained chord into a pumping, rhythmic trance lead.

steps.action: Hold a chord and enable the 16-step trance gate to chop it into a rhythm.
steps.tip: Program a syncopated gate pattern rather than straight 16ths for movement.

steps.action: Add unison detune and stereo spread for width, and set a moderate filter cutoff to keep it bright.
steps.tip: Automate the cutoff up over 8 bars for a classic build.

steps.action: Add a medium hall with mid/side width so the reverb sits around the dry lead.
steps.tip: Shorten the bass-multiplier so the low mids stay clear.

title: Lo-Fi Drum Bus
goal: Give a clean drum loop dusty, glued lo-fi character.

steps.action: Add a little squarize (bit reduction) and puncher for stepped digital grit.
steps.tip: A small amount goes a long way - keep the transients readable.

steps.action: Push the bus into the soft clipper to round peaks and glue the kit.
steps.tip: Clip for tone and cohesion, not just loudness.

steps.action: Echo only the high band lightly for a tape-like ambience while keeping the low end dry.
steps.tip: Reverse or scale the delay for a woozier feel.

title: Acid 303 Line
goal: Program a screaming acid bassline with slides and grit.

steps.action: Write a mono pattern with slides and accents, morphing the oscillator toward saw.
steps.tip: Slides between notes are what make the 303 sing - use them sparingly for emphasis.

steps.action: Automate the filter cutoff and resonance across the loop for the classic acid squelch.
steps.tip: High resonance near self-oscillation gives the vocal 'wah'.

steps.action: Drive the line for grit, choosing the harsher A/B mode for aggression.
steps.tip: Distortion after the filter emphasises the resonant peak.

title: Shimmer Pad
goal: Build an evolving, angelic pad with a rising harmonic tail.

steps.action: Start from a soft additive pad and mask harsh upper harmonics for a smooth tone.
steps.tip: Slow the amp attack so chords bloom in.

steps.action: Use a large mode (Space or Lush) and dial in pitch-shifted feedback for the shimmer.
steps.tip: Keep the dry/wet mostly wet for an ambient bed.

title: Sidechain Pump without a Compressor
goal: Duck a pad in time with the kick using a peak follower.

steps.action: Insert a Peak Controller on the kick track so its level becomes an automation signal.
steps.tip: Set a short decay so the duck recovers between hits.

steps.action: Right-click the pad's volume and link it to the Peak Controller's peak output, inverted.
steps.tip: Inverted mapping means loud kick equals quiet pad - the pump.

title: FM Bell Lead
goal: Design a bright metallic bell and place it in space.

steps.action: Raise the Coarse ratio toward a non-integer value for a clangorous, bell-like tone and add Wave Brightness.
steps.tip: Add a little vibrato LFO so sustained notes feel alive.

steps.action: Add a bright plate/hall so the bell rings out.
steps.tip: Predelay-style separation keeps the attack clear before the tail.

title: Wide Master Polish
goal: Add width and instant polish to a finished stereo mix.

steps.action: Nudge side level up for width, checking mono compatibility as you go.
steps.tip: Keep the low end centred - widen the mids and highs.

steps.action: Apply gentle psychoacoustic widening that stays mono-safe.
steps.tip: Bypass and compare - width should not thin the centre.

steps.action: Blend in a mode (A or B) at a low amount for cohesion and loudness.
steps.tip: This is a finishing sheen, not a mastering chain.

title: Robot Vocoder Vocal
goal: Turn a spoken or sung phrase into a talking synth.

steps.action: Use the built-in Sytrus carrier and feed the voice as the modulator, tuning band contouring for intelligibility.
steps.tip: Play sustained chords on the carrier so words ring out.

steps.action: Add a tempo-synced stereo delay for space and rhythmic interest.
steps.tip: Filter the feedback so repeats sit behind the dry phrase.

title: Granular Texture Bed
goal: Turn a one-shot or vocal into an evolving ambient texture.

steps.action: Load a sample and stretch it far down with wide grain spacing for a shimmering cloud.
steps.tip: Automate Spacing for movement between smooth and stuttered.

steps.action: Send the grains into a long impulse-response space, shaping the tail's width with the envelopes.
steps.tip: A cathedral or FX impulse turns grains into an ambient wash.
